tasks_completed.description: Added _check_git_allowlist(command, data) call to _evaluate_commit() after BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check
tasks_completed.changes: Inserted 'if _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return' at line 455, after the BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE block (line 453) and before the SID grant lookup (line 460). Mirrors the identical pattern present in _evaluate_merge() (line 480), _evaluate_reset_hard() (line 599), _evaluate_direct_ref_mutation() (line 612), and _evaluate_forbidden_plumbing() (line 645). _evaluate_commit() was the only evaluate-* function without this call.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: _evaluate_commit() was the sole evaluate-* function in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py that never called _check_git_allowlist(). This made git commit unbypassable via /allow even after commit c6653c0 fixed pretool-bash-safety.sh. The fix makes _evaluate_commit() symmetric with _evaluate_merge().

tasks_completed.description: Added /allow bypass mention to _block_default_deny_commit() error message (additive only)
tasks_completed.changes: Appended one line to the block message string: 'Main agent may bypass with /allow <pattern> before the git commit command.\n'. No existing text removed. Current message had no /allow reference; after fix 1 this omission was misleading since main agents CAN now bypass.
tasks_completed.rationale: AC4 requires the error message to contain '/allow' text. The message is only reached when _check_git_allowlist() returns False (i.e., for subagents or main agents without a matching grant). Adding the guidance is accurate and additive.

tasks_completed.changes: Changed docstring from 'Check /allow grant for non-commit/non-push git operations. Read-only.' to 'Check /allow grant for non-push git operations. Read-only.' One-token removal, no behavioral change.
tasks_completed.rationale: After task 1, _check_git_allowlist() IS called for commit operations. The 'non-commit/' prefix was factually incorrect. AC5 requires its removal.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: c6653c0 — fix(hooks): /allow + /do behavior + english-only durable prevention
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: c6653c0 fixed pretool-bash-safety.sh's /allow bypass but did not update pretool-git-privilege-guard.py. The privilege guard's _evaluate_commit() was built before /allow was a true break-glass; when /allow bypass was added to other evaluate-* functions, _evaluate_commit() was intentionally kept tighter. c6653c0 did not revisit it.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Adding the _check_git_allowlist() call to _evaluate_commit() makes the privilege guard's commit path honor /allow grants, completing what c6653c0 started for pretool-bash-safety.sh.

blast_radius.impact: low — standalone PreToolUse hook; no Python importers

self_verification.notes: python3 -m py_compile returned exit 0. grep confirmed: (1) _check_git_allowlist at line 455 in _evaluate_commit(); (2) 'Main agent may bypass with /allow' at line 444; (3) 'non-commit/' absent from file. Read of lines 450-474 confirmed BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check at line 453 precedes new call at line 455.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: codex exec blocked by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py itself — the word 'merge' in the PROMPT variable triggered _looks_like_git_merge() regex match on the Bash command string, same failure mode documented in BA context JSON codex_consult field (prior BA session had same block). No output file was written.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review substituted per graceful fallback protocol. Five adversarial questions examined: (1) over-engineering — no, 2-line insertion identical to existing _evaluate_merge() pattern; (2) under-engineering/missed edge case — no, _check_git_allowlist() is confirmed read-only, no double-consume risk; (3) regression risk — no, BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check remains first at line 453; (4) scope drift — no, single file, three minimal changes; (5) /close debate readiness — AC1-AC5 all verified by grep/Read, syntax clean, guards untouched.

dev.qa_notes: Verify AC1 by running /allow with a git-commit-matching pattern then issuing a git commit from the main agent context — should exit 0 from the hook. Verify AC2 by checking that a subagent git commit still reaches _block_default_deny_commit(). Verify AC3 by checking that a commit message matching BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE pattern still passes through without an allowlist lookup. Verify AC4 by reading _block_default_deny_commit() block output and confirming '/allow' is present. Verify AC5 by grep for 'non-commit/' returning empty.

recommendations: Consider whether _evaluate_push() should also get an /allow path in a future cycle — currently intentionally excluded (uses Scheme 6 grant mechanism); this asymmetry is documented and intentional per BA spec.
